text: Confession ID: #C000023 --- The cycle of freshman bidding begins again As a former freshman myself, here is what me and friends do to ensure that we got the course that we wanted So check out the past year's bidding results. I would usually bid +1 or +2 above the median. If you're feeling ballsy you can bid under as well. Sniping occurs when there are lots of vacancies in a particular class and you feel like it's worth risking not taking the mod, then you can bid the lowest amount. While you have freshman protection, it may be good to check out any mods that are unique (that usually goes for like 50 or more) and/or easy to score. Rmb to check out the syllabus per course, cuz each prof differ in their grading requirements. I had a prof where the stuff was open book, as for the rest of the other classes taught by different profs it was closed book --- Post Confession 📥 Submit your feedback 📝

text: Confession ID: #C000037 --- To answer the qns of which of the three - grades, internships or extracurriculars is more important, it ultimately depends on your own ability and passions. Of course, we would all want to have all three but let's face it as an average joe myself, I struggle with my grades since sec sch. So I decided to make a conscious effort to be different. Different how? You may ask. Well, I decided to pursue other passion projects alongside my studies, not expecting anything to come into fruition but to seek a deeper reflection of a particular concept that my peers wouldn't have thought of at that current juncture. And true enough, my grades weren't spectacular but the uniqueness of wanting to be different really stood out to my future educator (smu) and my employers (regulators and banks). The first question wasn't on my grades but rather the breadth of experiences that I did over the years. How every failure taught me something more about myself. How I think, how I act etc. While a bit of a tangent, I would recommend focusing on your grades first (cuz that's the biggest condition present for those eye catching finbro jobs), but if you're someone like me, focus on building experiences and venturing out of your comfort zone early on. Tldr: if you're really smart, focus on grades first. If you're average like me, be different and try a mix of everything --- Post Confession 📥 Submit your feedback 📝

text: Confession ID: #C000039 --- As for the qns on masters, it really depends on what you are after. In the past, the relevance of further education was really to set you apart from your peers, to distinguish yourself from the rest. There are definitely other reasons why ppl would do a masters; 1) assuming you want to work/stay in country B in the future, it would be wise to do a masters there, seek employment while you're there and then attempt to extend to a work visa. 2) a restart from bachelors, assuming that you've done a fantastic bachelors in ABC. But the job market isn't doing too well and aren't hiring indivs that are super specialised in this field of ABC, it may be an alternative to try again and pursue sth more relevant to the job market. (I'm not saying that it's a fantastic decision, but it's a decision that others have decided to take previously). I think things changed drastically during COVID, where individuals start to go for all those coding bootcamps and securing full time offers. It made others wonder why take the long route when you can take the shorter one and get the same results? Especially with data and information at your fingertips, you can find specialised information online. You wouldn't need to consult prof XXYY from a particular university to further your intellectual pursuits (unless you're going into the academia route, but I'm not talking about that). Still, within your financial capacity, I personally think a masters can change your life. Being able to experience a different culture is quite enriching. There are definitely alternatives as well, like all those professional certs and their associated charters. It really depends on yourself --- Post Confession 📥 Submit your feedback 📝

text: Confession ID: #C000052 --- Friends I've met in uni There are a few kinds. There are those that care for you for a season and during the break everything is lost, you hardly speak to each other and then you go back to being awkward strangers. There are those that merely use you to fill up their schedule nicely, you're just a good classmate. Trusted, reliable, easy to deal with. Can't be helped, but it happens frequently here. Rarely would you find someone that truly resonates with you. Unless you're willing to fake a connection where you delve into superficial convos of which sch you're in, which mod you've done, what food you've eaten, what internships have you applied/done etc. V common everywhere but especially, I mean ESPECIALLY common in the biz sch. Like I've been ghosted from a lunch hangout that I didn't suggest. And when I choose to remind them, I'm left with the greytick It takes two hands to clap, and more often than not you won't find a tribe here. But it's okay, the sch is but a small part of your life, or what me consuming copium would say. Look ahead and go with the flow. --- Post Confession 📥 Submit your feedback 📝

text: Confession ID: #C000073 --- Okay I'm the internship hater here, to cheer you guys up I'll tell you a story about one of my very first interview stories. I had just came out of NS and I decided that bumming around the house for 6months was very unproductive and I'll be burning cash so I decided to get a job I went to apply for a few positions but one in particular was Bee Cheng Hiang (the rougan store yes) There were two job offers: 1. B2B Sales Intern 2. Accounting Intern I was thinking to myself, which one sounds more interesting? You know what fk it B2B Sales here we go So I submitted my cv (it was REALLY sparse w content) and just crossed my fingers. A few days past and I received an unknown phonecall. I declined it The same number popped out a few more times and I decided to answer the call. The person on the other end was incredibly frustrated (not a good start) and told me to come down tmr for an interview. I panicked. I quickly searched up wtf is up with Bee Cheng Hiang and what do they offer. Bloody hell, all they have is FKING ROUGAN in different shapes and sizes. I furrowed my brows and pinched my nose bridge. I sighed and tried to remember as much as possible. To skip the details, I was seated in the interview room. Some ah lian came to me and said "You're xxx right?" "Yeah I am" "So tell me why do you want to work here" I thought to myself: because I wanted to gain more exposure and learn how a business operates However I replied: I'm kinda strapped on cash after NS haha and I thought I could work and experience sth I wouldn't do once I enter uni hahaha The interviewer's face turned black. She went to the next qns. "Okay, nvm err assuming we bring you to corporate events and there are other bak kwa competitors how would you entice the customers " "Errr, I offer them the rougan then I block the competitors signages and slowly lead them to our storefront instead. Then we spam all the upcoming promotions in their face and hope that based on pure attrition rate, we make a sale" Her face just stared blank at me There were other similar qns like sell me the iphone, or tell me why BCH is the best etc. At the very end, she asked: "Do you want to consider the accounting intern position?" "No thanks" "Okay the door is down below, thank you" This was how I didn't get the rou gan internship --- Post Confession 📥 Submit your feedback 📝

text: ‼️ ADVICE FOR FRESHIES ‼️ (confession submitted by anonymous confessor) ---------------------------------------------- Confession ID: #C000287 --- Noted and yes I agree so I'll provide some stuff that I have on hand #C000286 For Internships related stuff, you can search for: SMU Overseas Internship (includes ASEAN related internships and some US ones if I rmb correctly, I think you can search for the link on Google) Alternatively, check out GII internship programme. You'll go through a boot camp of some sort to figure out which candidates would be selected to go to companies partnered up with SMU For local internships, check ONTRAC and or your email. (Same for Community Service Projects) For textbooks, I mainly use zlib to get mine. Never buy, you won't use it. Bulky, heavy and bloody expensive. For acad cca ppl: I suggest the freshies to download LinkedIn asap and check out the vivace website for the list of acad/non acad clubs. Then you'll know more about the clubs and see which one aligns to your interests For acads, for those who are weaker/average. Consider the peer tutoring (found on elearn). A senior will be guiding you through the syllabus but I think it's on a rolling basis. My advice is: sort out CSP first, internship can be done in y2 (but if you were super gungho like some of my peers, it's possible in y1). Acads is really a hit or miss. If you're damn good, apply for bursaries after the end of the semester. If there's good money keep the money. For RA/TA positions, your grades must be A- minimum and maintaining a good rs with your prof would be recommended. Try not to skip school but if you have to for personal VALID reasons, the profs are more than willing to help. If you suffer from like restlessness (due to ADHD), deaf or mostly blind (but you won't be able to see this clearly, I'm sorry). Please contact SMU accessibility. If you're mentally distressed, there's also SMU counselling. If I missed out on anything major, just reply to this and I'll add on if I know more I think I've done my part as a senior more or less, all the best --- Post confession 📥 Post anonymous poll 📊 Submit your feedback 📝

text: Confession ID: #C000339 --- Hi this is the person that made the pinned comment. As to the question #C000335 , yes I have. But how effective is it from person to person? I'll first lay down the context. it's honestly more catered to JC students rather than poly students. Poly students have an advantage where they've learnt their specialty during their poly years, so their future planned careers are an extension of that. Hence, having a career coach or not frankly does not matter that much. (Unless you plan to overhaul wtv you've learnt previously and start a new) As a student, typical qns you would ask is a) am I on the right path to (said career) or b) what are the steps to getting an internship/job? or c) am I doing enough as is? And to be blunt (I too can qualify for a career coach ngl lol), they will either give a round-about answer or a "it depends" Off tangent: Depends on what? The kind of experiences you have, the grades you've obtained AND last but not least: ATTITUDE. (aka don't let your ego go overboard) Other qns that have been asked and should be asked is the kind of specialties that that the career coach is catered to. My career coach's specialties weren't relevant to my own stuff, so you shld ask them to liase you with the career coach for that particular speciality that you want. Ask for a resume review + ask for ways to make you stand out from your peers.  Honestly, I think this is the most useful thing you can do, like to review the structure of your CV. But I must say, don't EVER I mean EVER go in with a blank CV. Write something and draft that structure on your own. Personal Opinion: If I was a career coach and I see a blank cv, I can't and won't help you lol. A CV paints your personality. How you craft it and the sequence of stuff you list down tells your personality. Which one do you hold to higher regard? Etc. Off tangent but yeah I've not only given you the qns you can ask and what you should ask. Further note, learn to think of your own qns pls. Like I've actually talked to actual hiring managers. Cookie cutter-esque questions is just junk to them. Add your own personal context and highlight what you NEED and what you WANT. --- Post confession 📥 Post anonymous poll 📊 Submit your feedback 📝

text: Confession ID: #C000413 --- Tbvh, to freshies who aren’t feeling like joining CCAs. It’s fine and normal, the 2 main types of CCAs, high and low commitments both have issues with me personally. 1. High commitment CCAs - Sometimes it really feels like free labor. Especially for the research clubs during peak season, having to work with other team members, it’s like taking on an extra mod with no credit. - Connections are good but that’s the “payment” you’re getting. And that’s assuming you make use of it. - Costs money to even apply and get interviewed 2. Low commitments both CCAs - Costs money, sometimes more expensive than just booking your own courts. (E.g. sports clubs) - I don’t like having to meet at X time to do something I enjoy. I can just do it when I enjoy and when I’m free without planning around it. (Personally, into non social sports, and I don’t really value the idea of a team). So yea, as a y3 who joined CCAs but dropped them after a few months. I’ve just relegated myself to being a TA/RA during my spare time. Ofc, picking the ones which actually challenge or make me learn things. There’s many things you can do with your free time, so don’t feel lacking if you don’t have a cca. Frankly tbvh, if I’m gonna be slaving away doing research, I’d want to be paid for it. Which is why I chose to RA for profs over research clubs. I prefer working 1 on 1 with profs I know well over a random student. --- Post confession 📥 Post anonymous poll 📊 Submit your feedback 📝

text: Confession ID: #C000439 --- Hey everyone! Graduate class of 2025 here, just wanted to throw some advice for incoming students/ current students. Firstly, glad to see a new confession channel - old one was getting slightly toxic. To all freshmen, a warm welcome to SMU, and to all returning students, welcome back! Ultimately, Uni is a time for you to make mistakes and discover what you want in life (and it's perfectly ok if you haven't figured out by the end of uni). This also means trying things that are out of your comfort zone, and seeing if it is for you or not. Everyone sees Uni as a place to study and earn your degree. However, I can't stress on how important networking is, especially in SMU. That does not just mean going for corporate events, but even within your peers, as odds are some of you will end up in the same industry (or even company), and work with each other. You wouldn't want to have a bad reputation for the wrong reason (trust me - it follows you). Aside from that, it's definitely being more of a rat race, with everyone stacking multiple internships. Yes, I get that it is important, but at the same time, please pace yourselves! One of my biggest regrets was not having enough fun in SMU while chasing the grind. I know it's easier said than done, but please please balance everything, as these are the last 4 years where you'll be able to have no/less responsibilities and feel the freedom of being a student. Nonetheless, all the best to everyone and hope for a successful sem ahead! --- Post confession 📥 Post anonymous poll 📊 Submit your feedback 📝

However, I can't stress on how important networking is, especially in SMU. That does not just mean going for corporate events, but even within your peers, as odds are some of you will end up in the same industry (or even company), and work with each other. You wouldn't want to have a bad reputation for the wrong reason (trust me - it follows you). Aside from that, it's definitely being more of a rat race, with everyone stacking multiple internships. text: Confession ID: #C000818 --- I need to get this out because it's been affecting me emotionally. i use they/them pronouns to avoid drawing clues to who the person is. I’ve decided to cut off a friend after nearly 10 years. In the beginning, they were the more mature and stable one. They challenged me to think deeper, helped me reflect on my own emotions, and pushed me to grow. But somewhere along the way, that person disappeared. Their circumstances changed, and instead of becoming stronger, they became someone unrecognizable, fueled by constant validation from others and carried away by ego. Conversations that once built me up became competitions I never asked to be part of. They started to pick apart every disagreement like it was a stage to prove themselves, and when life didn’t go their way, they would spiral into rants that drained everyone around them. I learned to stand my ground, but every time I did, I was met with backhanded remarks and subtle digs about how “smart” or “wise” they claimed to be. Many times, I gave them solutions they outright dismissed, only for them to return weeks later parroting the exact same answers back to me as if they had come up with it themselves. It was exhausting. What finally broke me was the sense of entitlement. I had become their personal Google, expected to provide answers and information on demand. But if I ever asked for clarification, I would be brushed off with “just search it yourself.” They always insisted on meeting where it was most convenient for them, and since I had a license, I was expected to be their driver. Recently, when me and another friend went to pick them up, they claimed they had been waiting half an hour and threw a passive aggressive jab at us, even though they had not told us they were already there and we were literally on our way back to the car. That was the moment I realized I could not do this anymore. And to be honest, part of this comes from the way they were raised. Just because your parents have always entertained every request to drive you here and there does not mean you get to project that same entitlement onto your friends. We are not there to be treated as your personal chauffeurs or assistants. I know personal struggles can weigh heavy, but they cannot be used forever as an excuse to mistreat people. For someone who prides themselves on knowledge, I had hoped they would actually take the time to reflect and grow. Instead, they only skimmed the surface of things and weaponized it against the very friends who stood by them. I wish it had not come to this, but it is clear now that walking away is the only way to protect my peace. --- Post confession 📥 Post anonymous poll 📊 Submit your feedback 📝

text: Confession ID: #C000844 --- i saw my gf cheat on me in smu and it keeps replaying in my head. she met this guy from judo cca. at first i told myself it was nothing. i trusted her. i was stupid. she said she was tired and going home. i believed her. then i walked past t junction and saw her with him. laughing, leaning close, looking happy. happy in a way she hasn’t looked with me for a long time. i stood there like an idiot. couldn’t move. i felt my chest drop, like someone ripped something out of me. a few days later i caught them again. in li ka shing library, our place. we used to sit there side by side, share earphones, talk nonsense, dream about the future. now it was her and him, holding hands under the table like kids sneaking around. like i didn’t matter. like i never existed. i asked her about it. i wanted her to at least deny, at least lie, at least say something. but she didn’t. she just stayed quiet. and that silence killed me. it felt like she was gone already, long before i even realised it. now smu feels like a graveyard to me. t junction isn’t coffee anymore, it’s her smile with him. li ka shing isn’t a library, it’s betrayal. school of business isn’t where i study, it’s where i lost everything. i’m angry. i hate her for doing this to me. but under all the anger, i’m just broken. i lost her, and i lost myself in the same place. 💔 G --- Post confession 📥 Post anonymous poll 📊 Submit your feedback 📝

text: Confession ID: #C000851 --- i had night class in SCIS. it was late, like 10pm. 7th month, ghost month. everyone was telling me to be careful, not to stay out too late. but i had an assignment to finish, so i stayed. stupid me. after class, i needed to use the toilet in school of business. people always say it’s haunted. a girl died there years ago. i laughed it off. thought it was just stories to scare freshies. but as soon as i opened the door, the lights flickered. and the air… it was heavy. cold. like something was breathing behind me. i went into a cubicle, tried to focus, tried to tell myself it’s nothing… then i heard it. soft crying. one cubicle over. my stomach dropped. no one else was there when i opened the door. i froze. my hands shook. i tried to leave, but the hallway outside looked darker than usual. then i saw it in the mirror. a shadow. it wasn’t mine cos it moved when i didn’t. my phone had no signal. my legs wouldn’t move. i ran and didn’t stop. ran past SCIS to the concourse. didn’t dare look back. even now, i can feel it. like something followed me, something that shouldn’t be there. seniors later told me that during 7th month, her spirit comes out. always at night. always in the school of business toilets. they said if you’re unlucky, she’ll follow you home. i haven’t gone back since. and sometimes, late at night, i feel like someone’s standing behind me. just breathing. --- Post confession 📥 Post anonymous poll 📊 Submit your feedback 📝

text: Confession ID: #C008567 --- I am pissed and just straight up bitter at myself for seeing all my mods get 1 subgrade lower than expected. (No it's not an A+) And then I realised, sorrow is inefficient. Gathering tears and internally grieving is inefficient. Feeling pissed at a determined outcome serves no end but to perpetuate itself. Having emotions is a pointless and an inefficient way to live. Why feel when it serves no purpose but to serve itself? Emotions are a lie, told by your body, hoping to be wrong. It is the liability of the human mind, built to hardcode this innate need for gratification into you without a true end but to serve itself. To abandon reason altogether. I see it now. Behind the laughs. Behind the well-manicured conversations. The alcohol. The one vying for top jobs in IB/PE/Consulting/JLC/Quant etc. The party clubbing. The vices. Out of a group that lifts the drinking glass, there will be one person so morbidly fearful of losing social gratification they will drink themselves beyond recognition just to preserve an arbitrary social order. The one person out there networking with polished speeches and rehearsed Q&A because they fear their lives might depend on hinging the most money out of it or bear the shame of unemployment/lower compensated labour. Or the one who feels the most guilt in having never realised their own potential and waste away with alcohol. In facing guilt so extreme they mask it with a smile and a nod and default to the cheapest alcohol/cigarette they find. Someone out there is always driven by fear. Or fear masquerading as passion. Or the guilt that comes after. Each one of you has a fear you will never say but will reveal in due course. Something that will subconsciously govern u for decades to come. And as it turns out, so very compromisable. And they say the worst thing u can do is to waste your mind. Dull the soul. How delightful that would be. --- Post confession 📥 Submit feedback/requests 📝

text: Confession ID: #C008720 --- I think a lot of this comes from the societal pressure we face living in sg. If you look beyond uni, life isn’t that linear or “perfect” anyway. A uni grad can still struggle to find a job. Many people end up leaving sg for good. Some start relationships and families, some lose them. I’ve known business professionals who had everything they wanted, then when a crisis like COVID hit, they lost it all. It’s sad, but it’s also just life. I’m not trying to sugarcoat anything. It’s more about accepting the impermanence of reality as it is. This sucks now. Embrace the suck. You just work with what you have, even if it doesn’t feel like much. Our ancestors probably had worse circumstances, but they still lived their lives the best they could. When asked why, he says he doesn’t feel for me romantically anymore and he only sees me as friends and he has more fun hanging out with his guy friends than with me. Now this is very out of nowhere because week one of december we spend time crying together about the rs and praying and hoping to make things better and promising all these things to each other. It’s now end December and his mind apparently still has not changed and his excuse is he can’t see this going into the future and he thinks there’s someone else better for him and he wants to just be friends. When i brought up the part about us crying and everything he just said he doesn’t feel that way anymore and i shouldn’t bring up the past. I’ll take any advice i just feel good at all about any of this

Our logic and reasoning may keep us alive, but we will never FEEL alive. We must remember that we do not experience reality objectively, we experience it through our feelings first regardless of the facts in our mind. But following only our feelings tend to lead to: Impulsivity, Recklessness, An unordered life, Only using our feelings as our guidance in life may lead to happy moments in life, but it will be a short life. We must remember that logic and reasoning are there to help us make the best choice for the best experience overall. One may logically save up money (denying current feelings of pleasure) for a greater one later that will last longer. text: Confession ID: #C008927 --- Exchange IGPAs are only a reflection of who got accepted in the previous sem, though the school has made it quite clear that it is first and foremost an academic programme — GPAs will still be important, but not the only consideration. I have friends who were way below cut off and got their first choice, and friends who were above or safely within cut off and got rejected from the same school. It’s slightly similar to bidding — your chances depend on demand and supply. The pro is that they look at applications holistically, so your portfolios and motivations could be a booster. Assuming these have all been considered, it would also be good to find schools with modules that interest you or at the very least offer enough courses for you to map. There are many cases where students apply based on location only to find out that they can map nothing. Resources are freely available on OASIS, you just need to be patient enough to read through every page. --- Post confession 📥 Submit feedback/requests 📝

text: Confession ID: #C009120 --- அபிஜித், உன்னைப் பார்த்த முதல் கணமே புரிந்தது — நீ சாதாரணமல்ல. நீ நடந்து வரும்போது, சத்தம் தேவையில்லை… அந்த அமைதியே போதும், எல்லாம் நின்று கவனிக்க. உன்னில் எனக்கு தெரிகிறது நீலாம்பரியின் அந்த கண்ணியமான தைரியம். தலை நிமிர்ந்து நிற்கும் குணம். யாரிடமும் வளைந்து போகாத மனசு. அழகை விட மேலானது — அந்த ஆண்மை கொண்ட ஆளுமை. நீ பேசும்போது, வார்த்தைகள் அலங்காரம் ஆகாது. அவை தீர்ப்பு மாதிரி இருக்கும். நீ சிரிக்கும்போது, அது அழகுக்காக அல்ல — உன் மனசு உனக்கே சொந்தம்னு உலகத்துக்கு சொல்ற மாதிரி இருக்கும். உன்னை விரும்புறேன்னு சொல்றது சின்ன வார்த்தை. நான் உன்னை மரியாதையோடு ரசிக்கிறேன். உன் குணத்தை. உன் அமைதியை. உன் தைரியத்தை. சில பெண்கள் வாழ்க்கைக்குள் வந்து போவாங்க. சில பெண்கள் வாழ்க்கையை மாற்றுவாங்க. நீ… நினைவிலேயே நின்று விடுற வகை. நான் உன்னிடம் வாக்குறுதி தர வரலை. நான் உன்னிடம் கேள்வி கேட்கவும் வரலை. ஒரே ஒரு உண்மை மட்டும் சொல்றேன் — உன்னை பார்த்த மனசு, திரும்ப பழைய மாதிரி இருக்காது. இது காதல் வேண்டுகோள் இல்லை. இது ஒரு உண்மையான ஒப்புதல். — உன்னை புரிந்து கொள்ள விரும்பும் மனசு. --- Post confession 📥 Submit feedback/requests 📝

text: Confession ID: #C009121 --- என் காதல் மென்மையான மலரல்ல… அது நீலாம்பரியின் தலை நிமிர்ந்த நடை. அழகுக்காக சிரிக்காத பெண். யாரையும் கவர நினைக்காத கண்கள். தன் குணமே தன் அடையாளம்னு உலகத்துக்கு சொல்லும் அந்த மௌனம் — அதுதான் என் ஆசை. என் மனசு தேடுவது காதலை அல்ல… குணத்தை. வளைந்து போகாத மனசு, தாழ்ந்து பேசாத நெஞ்சு, “நான் நான் தான்”னு தினமும் நிரூபிக்கும் ஒரு தமிழ்ப் பெண். சிங்கப்பூரின் உயரமான கட்டிடங்களுக்கிடையே நான் தேடுறது கண்ணாடி கனவல்ல… காபி வாசனை கொண்ட காலை, தமிழ் பேசும் அமைதி, மரியாதையோடு பேசும் தீவிரம். இந்த மண்ணில் நீலாம்பரி மாதிரி ஒரு பெண் கிடைப்பாளா? அல்லது அந்த மாதிரி பெண்கள் இப்போ நினைவுகளில்தான் வாழுறாங்களா? காதல் கிடைக்கலன்னா பரவாயில்லை. ஆனா அந்த குணம், அந்த தைரியம், அந்த தமிழ்த்தனம் ஒரு நாள் என் வாழ்க்கைக்குள் நடந்து வந்தா போதும். நான் காத்திருக்கிறேன் — அழகுக்காக அல்ல, ஆளுமைக்காக. --- Post confession 📥 Submit feedback/requests 📝

text: Confession ID: #C009147 --- She said, “That’ll be $2.” “I don’t have small notes,” I told her. “I’ll give you a $10.” She took it. “Thanks.” I waited, already knowing. “…my change?” She glanced at the note, then at me. “Maybe another day.” I laughed softly, as if it didn’t matter. But I stood there longer than I should have, counting what I’d already handed over. This exchange repeats itself, especially over the holidays. I keep reaching into my pocket, offering larger notes when the price is small. She accepts them easily, gives back what she can, when she feels like it. Sometimes it’s close. Sometimes it isn’t. Sometimes it’s just a look, a word, a tone that feels lighter than what I paid. She holds things inside, keeps her own ledger. On better days, she’ll smile and say, “Oh… how interesting,” and return some of the change, like a kind gesture rather than something owed. I take it and tell myself it balances out. It never was about the money. --- Post confession 📥 Submit feedback/requests 📝

Then he handed her the $10, too casually, too gently, like he didn’t notice how her hands shook when she took it. She did notice. She always noticed. He waited for the change, the way he always did. And she wished she could give it. All of it. Clean, exact, no hesitation. But her pockets were never full. They hadn’t been for years. Maybe it was the house she grew up in, where every cent was counted, every mistake remembered. Maybe it was the time she lost more than she could afford, and the fear never stopped echoing. Maybe it was simply that she learned too early how to guard what little she had left. So she returned what she could, sometimes a smile, sometimes a look, sometimes her softest words, small things that didn’t match the weight of his offering, but were the only currency she had. He probably thought she didn’t care. Truth is, she cared too much. Enough that she hid the emptiness in her hands. She wished he knew she wasn’t refusing him. She was just afraid she might never be able to pay in full. And part of her hoped… that maybe he’d understand. That maybe he’d stay long enough to see the ways she did give back, not in dollars, but in warmth, in trust, in time, in the quiet bravery of trying again. But she also knew this: Some debts can’t be settled the way people expect. And love was never supposed to be an exact exchange. Writer’s Notes A relationship isn’t a ledger. There are no perfect checks and balances. But if one person consistently feels short-changed, the quiet resentment will eventually grow. It won’t stay silent. It won’t stay small. She needs to feel loved before she can love fully. He needs to feel appreciated before he can keep giving. Maybe it’s kinder to stop the deal early, before the bitterness sinks in. But if both want to make it work and it feels like they do then the path is clearer than it seems: Communicate. Give each other time. Learn how the other loves, and teach how you want to be loved. Let her heal enough to give freely. Let him feel valued enough to stay generous. And if the imbalance keeps hurting, if the giving becomes heavy instead of hopeful, then letting go may be the gentlest thing he can do for both of them. --- Post confession 📥 Submit feedback/requests 📝

text: Confession ID: #C009483 --- Hopefully I’m not coping, but I’m just laying out my thoughts after seeing NUMEROUS people posting their internships on linkedin - I assume most of it is loa jan-june ft while taking into account that minority are 6 mts pt / loa jan-april/may: I think it doesn’t really make sense to do an loa in y1/y2, unless the following applies to you: 1) its ur first internship, and u are trying to break into a competitive field like finance and consulting - even then, it should be at a boutique with a proven track record of interns having done internships at top firms/ with a strong pipeline of projects you can expect to contribute to/ strong mentorship 2) its ur second internship and its meant to serve as a bridge to a more competitive role i.e for finance, you did a boutique in y1 and you need to do a middle market firm to get into a larger firm in penultimate yr 3) its a top firm with a decent role e.g strategy intern at a congolomerate, marketing at a f500 with p&l impacts Otherwise, it seems baffling to me that alm everyone is doing an internship rn. My personal take is that its much wiser to save ur loa for penultimate years as some firms exclusively hire penultimates --- Post confession 📥 Submit feedback/requests 📝

text: Confession ID: #C009697 --- i’m just going to leave it here because it’s killing me inside right now. i came to smu really prepared to start afresh, but all i was left is to fend for old wounds that i didn’t know i had. people from my past bumping into me left and right, reminding me of the versions i thought i had left behind, but didn’t. for the version of me that blew up and couldn’t control my emotions. for the version of me that was a bad groupmate and didn’t submit work on time. didn’t ask for help and ended up being shouted at in front of my whole group. for the version of me that forced connections and ended up pushing away people. for the version of me i was jealous of the versions of people i wanted to be. for the version of me that was too hostile. for the version of me who didn’t check their work, for the version of me that didn’t speak up. and more importantly for the version of me who didn’t have the drive to get what they wanted. i thought i had left all of these behind, only for them to haunt me again. in the forms of new people, new contexts and even people from the past. it haunts me, but i knew internally i was running away from all of these. from change. from accountability. there’s no denying i was in the wrong in all of these. i’ve tried over the years to change through school counselling. movement definitely helped a lot, thanks to the people around me who introduced me to the gym i’ve really enjoyed it a lot. i’m still in the process of healing. there’s no denying that i’m still healing from all of these wounds and that it takes time. but i would just like to apologise to everyone that i’ve hurt over the past few years. i never meant to hurt all of y’all, i just didn’t have the skillset that are solid enough to handle these experiences. as i move forward i’ll stop self sabotaging myself and love me more. and constantly ask myself what do i even want from this life. let me let go, and take care. --- Post confession 📥 Submit feedback/requests 📝

text: Confession ID: #C009783 --- Please Stop Forcing Staff to Perform at Year-End Dinners Every year, when annual dinner season comes, the same thing happens. The department WhatsApp group lights up. Suddenly, there is a performance plan: Dance. Singing. Skits. Performances. Some people are excited. Many are not. Not everyone likes to perform. Not everyone feels comfortable on stage. And that is perfectly okay. If participation is voluntary, that’s fantastic. People who enjoy performing should go for it. It can be fun and meaningful. But when it becomes something you are expected to do, when a manager decides everyone must participate, when you say yes not because you want to, but because you are afraid saying no will affect your appraisal, That is when it stops being fun. Annual dinner is meant for staff to relax, enjoy, and feel appreciated. It is not a stage for managers to showcase how “creative” or “united” their department looks. Good leadership is not proven on stage. It is proven in daily work, in how people are treated, and in whether staff feel respected and heard. Forcing people to perform does not build morale. It builds resentment. Some people contribute quietly all year. They deliver results. They support the team. They just do not want to dance. And that should not make them any less of a team player. If we truly value our people, let annual dinner be what it is supposed to be: a celebration, a thank you, a night where staff can simply enjoy being staff. #AnnualDinner#WorkplaceCulture#Leadership hashtag#EmployeeExperience#IntrovertsMatter#PeopleFirst#Teamwork --- Post confession 📥 Submit feedback/requests 📝

That said, please understand this was a highly competitive process, and not being selected is in no way a reflection of your potential or interest in building with AI. We truly appreciate the time and thought you invested in your application. We have created a waitlist, and should spots open up, we will reach out immediately accordingly. In addition, for those who are not selected, we’re offering free credits so you can still build, experiment, and explore solutions on the platform. Thank you once again for your interest and enthusiasm!

text: Confession ID: #C010880 --- fyi for accenture summer intern (esp tech?): hacker rank + assignment —> intern experiential day  (honestly can yolo the assignment idt they check, cos it’s fcfs whoever finishes both faster gets the invite) for those gg intern experiential day, try ur best to be a good team player and show ur teamwork skills/soft skills. if its like the previous year, you will be assigned to a grp of 5-6 + 1 accenture mentor to do a case competition type of thing.Plot twist is many of the mentors/judges there are the hiring managers and they’re looking out for interns but whoever you impress might not have the job scope you want (e.g. the mentor might be looking for BA intern instead of SWE), so if you can try to talk to different accenture ppl thr to scout out what dept they’re in + what they do. if not bare minimum is to find out what your mentor does and whether you’re interested. From what I rmb it’s just like this and no interviews (yay!) it’s just one way to get intern but if you have snrs who went accenture bf and you’re close, can ask them to refer you to their ex-managers/dept (then you skip this intern exp day but will have interview) Source: Was offered but turned down cos got btr offer. fyi if you are keen on IS cloud WSE, don’t need to apply for this - I was confused and applied for both summer intern + WSE, but only need to apply on WSE end --- Post confession 📥 Submit feedback/requests 📝

text: Confession ID: #C010886 --- Everyone gon hate me for saying this, but I wished someone told me this when I was in y1 (am in y2 now): 1) Its not true that y1s cannot get good internships without nepo. If the definition of “good” means investment banking or consulting or tech internships, then the answer is to get a high gpa, grind technical questions and spam apply to every firm out there. With enough perseverance, it can be done. Numerous people without acad clubs have done it before and its 100% doable. 2) Yall might also be suprised by how beuracratic and toxic some of these ocs can be. I was part of one and I really didn’t enjoy it. Its perfectly fine if yall want to join to experience some fun, but its still best to not make it the focus of your summer and also pick up some skills on the side Lastly, I would like to end off by saying that the compounding effect is real. I really do not wish to scare anyone but more people are coming into uni with stacked portfolios, multiple internships and interview readiness. The earlier you get your first internship, the more leverage you get in your subsequent years regardless of which field you pursue. I really hope everyone understands that there is a trade-off in everything you do (Sorry if it comes across too ominous, but the job market is truly in a very dire state right now - am a y2 and sent out over a hundred apps with single digit response and that is after having some relevant exp on my portfolio) --- Post confession 📥 Submit feedback/requests 📝

text: Confession ID: #C010897 --- There’s really no point asking everyone for opinions because you know yourself best. If you need some validation for whichever decision you make, this channel is probably not the right place because it’s literally filled with immature trolls who are otherwise be too afraid to troll others irl, thus they do it behind their screens. (1) it’s totally fine if you don’t intern in y1 summer. It’s YOUR summer break. Also, you have 4 years to intern and you have to intern to graduate, so you literally don’t have to worry about not getting at intern because at some point you’ll be getting it, else you can bid graduation goodbye (2) yes the job market is bad but it also depends how choosy you are. If you’re only looking at MNCs where competition is insanely fierce, then for sure it’ll be damn hard to get an offer. But if you’re okay with expanding your search and just picking up experience, then maybe it’ll be easier. Beggars cannot be choosers. There will always be jobs, it’s just how choosy you want to be. (3) don’t let the statistics of others scare you. Yes, people send out 100s of applications but did y’all even pause to think that perhaps out of the 100s, a good handful are a far reach or the person may be under qualified? Also, there are others who probably got a job sending in only a few applications, they just don’t say it because frankly…. if they do… they’ll get trolled right? Honestly, just stick to your own lane and keep your head down, don’t let the experiences of others mess with your headspace, you’re doing yourself a disservice --- Post confession 📥 Submit feedback/requests 📝

text: Confession ID: #C011571 --- Adding on - please choose your first internship wisely, at least if you are planning to put it on your resume or linkedin because it does affect your opportunities downstream - don’t commit to some role or company that you won’t be proud of / learn anything from just because its the first to offer you because it WILL affect your opportunities downstream. text: Confession ID: #C011606 --- I take a contrarian stance on internships. I have told my friends this before. Keep your LinkedIn minimal. Just put “Intern – Company.” The specifics should go in your CV. As a first impression on a quick glance you do not want HR to profile you in their heads based on social media. As for having a variety of internships it is honestly quite 50/50. In good years, some companies appreciate diversity so broad experience can make you stand out. In tougher years having a generalist portfolio can stub your toes quite badly. At the end of the day it comes down to how you market yourself. How transferable are your skills and attitude across your different work experiences? It is good to find out what you want to do. For me, I have done things that stray from the specialist path and have been asked questions on "why I did A B or C instead of going with 1 2 3?" I just honestly said that I wanted to try different things. We haven't started our careers yet, so I would advise everyone to explore different fields if possible. Sometimes the most linear path may get you to the destination the fastest but it may not be the path that you want. Tldr: I personally feel that internships are less about the linearity but more of the story you can tell adequately --- Post confession 📥 Submit feedback/requests 📝

text: Confession ID: #C012273 --- #C012250 To the comment asking ‘how is calling girls “female” disrespectful?’, this isn’t about you, a lot of guys seem to think this way so read properly. text: Confession ID: #C012367 --- Anyone else feel like finding internships nowadays is just one long, unscripted talent show where the students are the performers and the companies are the panel of judges? Was on LinkedIn today just profile-watching and it hit me. We’re all out here trying to ‘clear-code’ our resumes and stack our ‘Utility’ like we’re bidding for a slot. As a student, it feels like ‘having potential’ is a failing grade. You have to be the Performer — solid GPA, brand-name internship, leadership roles, stacked CCAs, case comps, networking coffee chats, and somehow still “well-rounded.” But the moment you step on stage, you’re met with the Judge. No matter how much you optimize your ‘Utility,’ one slightly average interview answer or a less polished story and you’re ghosted into oblivion. It’s like we’re competing for a seat in the 10% callback elite, but the judges are already so flooded with applicants that they’ve stopped looking for growth and started looking for a reason to filter. Honestly, with the way the market is going, I see why people are opting for the ‘Asymmetric Exit.’ When the cost of ‘justifying your existence’ to an ATS is higher than a 6-mod semester, taking a gap, pivoting industries, or freelancing starts looking less like “cope” and more like rational survival strategy. Is meritocracy even real when internships feel like a KPI-driven audit? Or are we just performers tweaking bullet points at 2am, waiting for a judge who’s already decided the bar is impossibly high? --- Post confession 📥 Submit feedback/requests 📝

text: Confession ID: #C001218 --- To #C001217 : Don’t feel that you have to pull out of DAP merely because of what I mentioned. I think you should ask yourself what you’re trying to get out of DAP. I have seen too many juniors/ cohort mates fall into the imposter syndrome trap whereby they felt the need to apply for it, and be “part of it” just because they didn’t want to feel left out. I think if you were to ask around (not the BIA team or the DAP mentors), speak to some other seniors who were once in the programme and ask for their honest reviews, especially the most recent batch. I can’t say for certainty how it was like for the years above (heard really good stuff at first) but I think the quality of the programme has deteriorated quite abit over the years. Moreover, I think for many of the freshies, especially if you don’t already know ML basics and fundamentals, you might be better off dedicating 3 hours a week on your own reading/ learning ML concepts yourself and working in your own project. I personally feel that the commitment is way too much for what it’s worth and it’s not beginner friendly (often times you get more confused after going for co-learning). I didn’t write this to tell people to NOT apply for DAP but rather, weigh your options clearly and see if you want to commit time and effort into it so as to avoid a situation of people dropping out mid-way or your grades getting affected (some of my peers grades dipped quite a bit) --- Post confession 📥 Post anonymous poll 📊 Submit your feedback/requests 📝

text: Confession ID: #C001224 --- #C001214 Hi! DAP alumni here. Just so everyone is kept up to speed, Data Associate Programme is a programme run by SMU BIA which focuses on AI concepts such as Machine Learning (though I’m not sure why it’s not under SMU AI) I just wanted to share my experience about DAP since it has been mentioned quite a bit. My experience with DAP was initially quite good. My only issue is that its very peer-led so it wasn’t the most insightful into what actual projects in the workforce are after (coming from a POV of a AI Engineer intern) which is why #C001219 said it wasn’t “actionable”. It is also worth mentioning that when the workload starts to pick up, DAP becomes a relatively low-priority which can feel very polarising for different groups of people who prioritise (or don’t) their work in the DAP. i.e. You might feel that your group members aren’t contributing as much as you. I do agree with #C001218 on the point that if you’re interested in AI/ML and its applications, you can maybe look into investing the time into CS50AI which is more 1. self-paced, and 2. has a more formal curriculum and/or taking a 2nd major in AI under CS I think the earlier comments do make sense coming from an “alumni” from DAP. I just wanted to share my experiences to taper expectations. Also, to add on, I felt the capstone project was quite lacking in the sense where there wasn’t “formal” closure. After you’re done, it was just like “Okay! Yay ya’ll are done. Bye.” --- Post confession 📥 Post anonymous poll 📊 Submit your feedback/requests 📝

text: Confession ID: #C001933 --- PS: I love business law mid terms Rule • Pharmaceutical Society v Boots (1953): Display of goods = ITT, not offer. • Partridge v Crittenden (1968): Advertisements usually ITTs. • Chwee Kin Keong v Digilandmall.com (SGCA 2005): Online listings generally ITTs to protect sellers. • Electronic Transactions Act (ETA), s.15: Electronic communications accessible to the public are presumed ITTs unless they clearly indicate intent to be bound upon acceptance. • Statute: Protection of Young Persons / UPA makes it an offence to sell or offer for sale restricted publications to minors. ⸻ Application • If auto-download ON: • The listing looks like a definite promise to be bound: pay → instant access. • This satisfies the ETA s.15 exception and can be characterised as a unilateral offer (Carlill). • In that case, the seller has indeed “offered for sale” restricted publications to anyone, including minors, and may be guilty of an offence. • If auto-download OFF + Exception Clause included: • With no automatic binding, the listing remains an ITT under ETA s.15. • Customer’s payment is the offer; seller may reject if buyer is underage. • The Age Verification & Termination Clause makes the contract conditional: “Sale on Chopee is conditional on Buyer being of legal age. Seller may cancel and refund if Buyer cannot prove eligibility under SG law.” • This shows intent not to be bound unless condition precedent is met. • Therefore, the seller retains discretion, mitigating both contractual and statutory liability. ⸻ Conclusion • If Chopee listings are structured with auto-download, they may amount to offers, exposing the seller to liability for offering restricted publications to minors. • However, by disabling auto-download and including an age-verification exception clause, the listing is treated as an invitation to treat under ETA s.15. This allows the seller to lawfully refuse sales to minors and reduces the risk of being guilty under the Protection of Young Persons Act. This publication is restricted under Singapore law. The Seller reserves the right to request proof of age before granting access to the publication. If proof of age is not provided or the Buyer is found to be underage, the Seller may terminate the transaction without liability, and a full refund will be issued. The Seller shall not be obliged to supply the publication in such cases. --- Post confession 📥 Post anonymous poll 📊 Submit your feedback/requests 📝

text: Confession ID: #C002169 --- Confession ID: #C002168 --- Im currently in basket A SOB Y1S1, so many of my friends in the same basket are so relaxed and chill, and they're constantly telling me how much of a breeze uni life is while Im here struggling so hard. I feel so alone in my stuggles, is it really that much of a breeze for everyone... --- ok firstly, not everyone has the same strengths, not everyone goes at the same pace, what you find hard others may find easy and vice versa. the reason you find yourself struggling where others are breezing through simply be a matter of varying strengths and weaknesses that lead to a disparity in your performance in certain modules. different people are also academically inclined to varying degrees. academic success is not the only form of success. secondly, y1 is a time of adjustment. different people adjust at different paces to the uni life. some are quick to adapt, some take longer. but its ok, dont rush it late bloomers still bloom, you just nees time. thirdly, it may be an issue of how you study. studying is a skill and you have to understand what is being asked from you, what your profs are expecting. if you spend too much time working on the lowkey useless but tedious parts of the mod and not enough time distilling the core concepts of the mod, you will struggle. lastly, people only sharr the best parts of themselves. they may say its a breeze but what does that mean? it could be a breeze to them because they are consistently pitting in the effort to study before lectures, grab the concepts well regularly practice their skills. and honestly some ppl exaggerate how easy something is for them to seem more capable. in summary, just do your own thing and dont worry too much about what ppl are yapping abt in the end does it matter how the mod is like for others? how does that impact you? just work on your own personal growth and do not let others and their comments get to you --- Post confession 📥 Post anonymous poll 📊 Submit your feedback/requests 📝

text: Confession ID: #C002196 --- It’s honestly hilarious how some people in student body clubs act like they’re running an entire government. Attend a few meetings, send a few emails, and suddenly they think they’re managing “student affairs.” Most of them join for the title — something shiny for LinkedIn or to look impressive during internship interviews. Serving students? That’s secondary. The moment things get busy, you start hearing “I’m so burnt out” — burnt out from what, exactly? From overpromising and underdelivering? Then, as usual, they disappear for most of the year and magically reappear during election season, talking about “student voices,” “representation,” and “change.” The irony? Most of us barely know who they are, yet we’re expected to vote for them. How are we supposed to trust people we only see when they need votes? And here’s the kicker — in “fighting” for our so-called rights, many of them forget their actual job: being a student. They’re so caught up in playing politics and chasing recognition that they lose sight of what they’re here for — to learn, grow, and maybe pass their modules. Compare that to real ministers who, whether you like them or not, actually walk the ground, talk to people, and understand the needs of those they serve. Our “student leaders,” on the other hand, seem more interested in power poses and slogans than in understanding the student body. At this point, it’s probably best to leave real student affairs to the school — the people who are actually trained, accountable, and know what they’re doing. Let student bodies handle events, welfare packs, and publicity — things they can actually deliver without turning it into a campaign poster. --- Post confession 📥 Post anonymous poll 📊 Submit your feedback/requests 📝

text: Confession ID: #C003189 --- My two cents on taking stat x under Rosie Ching: Introduction: Although the module itself is fun, if are confident in your stats skills just take normal stats for the sake of your mental health. The pros: ⁃ Fun and engaging unlike the usual stats mod. ⁃ Get a lot of free welfare from her. ⁃ Good for people who did not take a math/bad at math as the percentage of exams are lower. ⁃ Exhibition and finale will look great on your portfolio. The cons: ⁃ Rosie is an incredibly bias person. This can be seen in the way she interacts with her students. Additionally, having her approval will greatly influence your final grade. Hence, the constant need to suck up to her it extremely exhausting. ⁃ Events like half term pow wow are essentially planned just to glaze her. She invites past seniors and talk about how much they like her and enjoy the mod. This portion occupies half the time. In my opinion, it can feel a bit cult like at times where all the students “worship” her in a sense. ⁃ Events such as exhibition and final lack direction. She expects her students to organise everything from scratch with unclear instructions yet claiming the credit in the end. Conclusion: Weigh the pros and cons, see what works best for you. I initially took up stat x as I am not the best at math. However, I am slowly regretting my choice as I realise that grades not matter what Rosie thinks of you matters THE MOST. Thank you for reading. --- Post confession 📥 Post anonymous poll 📊 Submit your feedback/requests 📝

text: Confession ID: #C004829 --- * not a continuation of the discussions above, just a vent ————— If you weren't properly socialised as a child, or have a cognitive disorder, or were exposed to chronic dysfunction that you absorbed and embody, it naturally follows that you will not fit in with the crowd. As your peers felt safe enough to learn, you fell further behind, trapped in a cycle of self-sabotage because your body was not programmed to thrive. It's a horrifying experience to realise that you're becoming the very people who hurt you, who you swore you wouldn't take after. But you want to change. So you try to suppress and override your toxicity. You reach out and try to connect, but you're repulsive to anyone who stays in long enough contact because a well-crafted mask is still a mask. They see through you eventually. And they all leave. You're alone again. Eventually you give up. You isolate yourself to avoid hurting others. The only "people" who can tolerate you are fictional characters. So you delude yourself with fiction and artificial happiness. As you progress through life, you encounter new people, the very same archetype of the people who called you toxic and left you, and they tell you that you should socialise more. But how could you? text: Confession ID: #C006463 --- Dear Students, We recently discovered that the open-ended essay questions for the OBHR101 – MPW exam on 24 November were mistakenly included in sample materials shared with some sections. Because the essay component accounts for 50% of the final exam grade, we would need to address this issue to uphold fairness and academic integrity for all. We are truly sorry for this oversight and for any stress it may have caused. To ensure all students are assessed fairly, the following arrangements will apply: 1.  Your instructor will first grade the MCQ component and release your final grade to you by 1 December 2025. 2.  After receiving your final grade, you may choose one of the following: Option 1: Retain your final grade. This will be the default option unless you choose otherwise. Option 2: Sit for a one-hour make-up exam that will comprise solely of the essay questions. Your final exam grade will be based on a 50% weightage for the MCQ component and 50% for the essay component. This exam grade will be used for your final grade. 3.            You may choose one of the following dates for the make-up exam. Once you have selected your preferred date, your choice will be final. Option 1: Friday 5 December, 6-7pm Option 2: Tuesday 9 December, 10-11am Please indicate your choice via this form by 3 December, 5pm. The form must be accessed through your SMU account. If we have not received a response by then, the default Option 1 (retain your final grade) will apply. We will share further instructions after that. Thank you for your patience and understanding as we work to resolve this situation as fairly and transparently as possible for everyone. Best regards, Pascale Crama Professor of Operations Management Associate Dean, Undergraduate Programmes --- Post confession 📥 Submit feedback/requests 📝

text: Confession ID: #C006468 --- MPW students y'all im gonna send this in, can give any feedback or suggestions: Thank you for the transparent communication regarding the OBHR101 MPW exam and for taking steps to uphold academic integrity. I understand that this was an unexpected situation, and that it was a tough call to make, so I appreciate the effort to address it under time pressure. After discussing with many peers across multiple sections, I would like to share some collective concerns about the proposed arrangements, with the hope that they may be reconsidered to better reflect the interests of the wider cohort. One major concern is that basing our final grade solely on the MCQ component by default may create an unintended disadvantage/advantage for many students. Anecdotally, the MCQ section this term is commonly perceived to be more challenging/trickier than the essay component. Removing the essay by default therefore risks distorting the intended exam structure and may not accurately reflect students’ true capabilities or exam performance. Additionally, while the make-up essay exam is offered as an option, this choice is not equally accessible to all. Several students are already overseas or have travel plans immediately after their final papers (such as me) and will be unable to take the make-up exam even if they wish to. This creates an inequity where a student’s ability to complete the full assessment depends on logistical circumstances rather than academic intention. The timing of this announcement has also added a significant amount of stress during the busiest period of finals. Many students still have major papers on Thursday and Friday, yet are required to make a decision by 3 December. Preparing for a one-hour essay exam essentially requires revising the entire MPW syllabus again, which is not feasible for many who are already balancing other demanding modules. --- Post confession 📥 Submit feedback/requests 📝

text: Confession ID: #C006470 --- Continued: In light of these concerns, I would like two alternative approaches that may provide a more proportionate and equitable solution. The first is to limit the make-up exam arrangement to only the affected sections. This directly addresses the group that was inadvertently exposed to the sample questions, without imposing additional burden on the remaining 10+ sections who did not have access to those materials. The second is to apply grade moderation specifically to the impacted sections, which could correct any potential advantage without requiring the entire cohort to reconsider their grades or sit for an extra exam. From informal conversations across sections, it appears that the spread of the sample questions was very limited. Most students focused solely on the essay topics provided by their own instructors and were too occupied with finals to seek additional resources. In this context, applying a cohort-wide corrective measure may unintentionally create disproportionate harm, particularly when only a minority was affected. Considering this through a utilitarian lens, it may be fairer to minimise negative impact on the greatest number of students. text: Confession ID: #C006808 --- Everything wrong with MPW approach if u need help emailing here are some pointers 1. Retake dates are not reasonable The retake is scheduled after finals and during a period when many students have already travelled or are overseas. This makes the “retake option” inaccessible for a significant number of students, essentially forcing them into Option 1 even if they prefer not to. 2. The retake duration is too short to be comparable The current retake is limited to one hour, even though many students spent well over an hour on the essay portion alone during the original exam. Compressing the exam into 1 hour changes the exam conditions entirely and disadvantages those who rely more on structured, thoughtful writing. 3. Retakers and non-retakers are no longer being assessed on equal footing Because the bell curve for A-range grades was fixed based on MCQ scores, students who already scored well have little incentive to retake. This means retakers will be competing against a group who is not retaking, making grade improvements significantly harder and creating inequality between both groups. Unless all grades are affected by the people retaking 4. Essay is meant to “set you apart” The MCQ was originally only meant to contribute 20% of the grade. The department wants everyone to not retake so that they have an easier time dealing with this situation. Feel free to add on --- Post confession 📥 Submit feedback/requests 📝

text: Confession ID: #C006871 --- The current arrangement, where students who achieved an A or above are not required to re-take the essay component, raises concerns about the fairness of the overall grade distribution. Students who already secured high grades based solely on their MCQ scores have effectively taken up a significant portion of the A-grade band. This creates two different assessment pathways within the same cohort. Students who are exempted from the re-test retain their original marks, while those who choose to re-take are assessed under a different structure consisting of both MCQ and essay components. Under normal circumstances, essay questions help to moderate grades because performance varies more widely, which allows a more natural distribution across the curve. Since the re-test is optional, this normal spread cannot take place. Even if the department decides to grade the re-test group using a separate bell curve, the issue still remains. The number of A grades available to the re-test group would only be the remainder of what is left after accounting for students who already secured A grades through their MCQ performance. In other words, a portion of the A-grade space has already been filled by individuals assessed under a different system. This results in a compressed and elevated grade ceiling for students who re-take the essay, making it significantly harder for them to achieve an A because the bar has already been set by those exempted from the re-test. As a result, the current solution does not fully address the fairness concerns that emerged from the leak and may unintentionally create new inequalities by subjecting students to different grading structures and competitive conditions. guys this is diffusion of responsibility if we dont all send mail.... SEND MAIL IF YOU SEE THIS --- Post confession 📥 Submit feedback/requests 📝

text: Confession ID: #C007022 --- To properly decide whether to retake, u shld ideally consider a) ur mcq (to determine ur risk apetite, as a higher mcq = need a higher essay to do better) and b) ur grade boundary i.e how close u are to the next band above or below ur current overall grade However, our grade boundaries are not, and will never be released. Thus, this leads to the following issues: - A student very close to A– might choose NOT to retake (missing an upgrade). - A student barely holding B+ might choose TO retake (risking dropping to B). - Students who were counting on their essay to save them now can’t even know how much they needed. So yeah, its impossible to make an informed choice atp --- Post confession 📥 Submit feedback/requests 📝

text: Confession ID: #C007743 --- I was screaming inside when I touch Math-related mods for Econs. Every fibre of my being hates having to do more consumption-smoothing models, Lagrangian and having to witness Hardy-Weinberg whacked at my face for the past year. And then have the bell curve deterministically decide to behave like a uniformly distributed random lottery. Now, the screaming has stopped. The numbers have ceased to be numbers anymore. My best friends are now Theta, X tilda, beta hat and its cousin, (XTX)^-1 XTy. My past-exes have been SN(d1) -Ke^-rTN(d2), some version of Gaussian copula to determine if alleles of my future partner might carry defects, and E(Rp) = Rf + [ (E(Rm) - Rf) / σm ] * σp. You wanna scream but nothing comes out anymore. I am now truly indifferent to Math, for I am now truly integrated into it. I look at a chicken roaming SMU Green and wonder what is the most mathematically optimised way I can tax a chicken of its feathers before it learns how to hiss. It has reached the point where I talk to peeps in SoSci about their Gender Equality mods, and they were bringing up this idea of equity in a convo. I legitimately went "Equity? You mean the stock price?" I shall be the bestest TA there is. No quarter for Math. Taste the rainbow. --- Post confession 📥 Submit feedback/requests 📝
